name: Cranberry-Apple Strudle
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 teaspoons cornstarch dissolved in 2 tablespoons cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, core, and quarter the apples. Cut each quarter in half, then cut each wedge into three pieces for apple chunks.
2. In a heavy-bottomed 12-inch frying pan, melt 3 tablespoons butter over medium-high heat. Add sugar and cook until melted and light brown with a peanut butter consistency.
3. Carefully add the apples to the saucepan, stirring constantly. Mix in cinnamon, allspice, and nutmeg evenly over the apples.
4. Cook the apple mixture until caramel melts and apples soften. Stir in cranberries and cornstarch mixture; cook 2-3 minutes for juices to thicken and glossy appearance.
5. Let the apple-cranberry mixture cool in a shallow dish.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line baking sheet with parchment paper.
7. Melt remaining butter, brush phyllo sheets one at a time, placing them on prepared sheet. Do not butter top sheet; reserve some melted butter for brushing the roll's top.
8. Place apple-cranberry mixture along the long side of phyllo strip about 1 inch from edge. Roll up tightly, ensuring it doesn't touch sheet sides. Brush the top with reserved butter and bake for 25-30 minutes until nicely browned.
9. Let cool at least 15 minutes before serving.
Per serving: 247 calories, 2 gm protein, 43 gm carbohydrates, 9 gm fat, 19 mg cholesterol, 5 gm saturated fat, 96 mg sodium
